<!-- Generated by the ps-soc-design workflow (7 agents, grounded in the RTL) +
     adversarially verified (the stalling verdict). The authoritative architecture
     spec for the PS-assisted KV260 SoC (#37-#41). -->

# Ventium KV260 PS-Assisted SoC — Unified Design & Implementation Plan

## 0. Architecture in one paragraph

The PL (xck26 fabric) holds **only** `ventium_top` = core + L1 + the AXI4 master to PS-DDR, exactly as already built and verified behind `+VEN_L1_AXI`. **All** peripherals (UART, PIT/PIC, IDE, VGA, keyboard, RTC) and the int-0x80 kernel are **emulated in PS A53 software** — the C++ device models / syscall emulator / reset sequencing of `verif/tb/tb_main.cpp` move onto the A53. The PL adds exactly **two new seams** to the proven `bd_l1axi.tcl` BD: (1) one **AXI4-Lite control/IO/syscall slave** on PS `M_AXI_HPM0_FPD`, and (2) one **PL→PS interrupt** (`pl_ps_irq0`) into the GIC. The existing on-die `rtl/soc/ventium_soc.sv` (devices in PL) is the opposite approach and is **not** used here.

---

## 1. The pivotal correctness question — resolved with RTL evidence

The cosim feeds `io_ack` and the proxy response with **zero latency**; real PS service takes microseconds. The two seams answer the "can the core stall?" question **oppositely**, and this asymmetry drives the entire plan.

### 1.1 Port-I/O (IN/OUT/INS): STALLS CLEANLY — no RTL change (adversarially verified)

The `S_IO` arm (`rtl/core/core_io.svh:22-41`) is `if (io_ack) begin ... end` with **no else**. When `io_ack==0`: `state` is not reassigned (holds `S_IO`, `always_ff`), `eip` not advanced, `gpr[R_EAX]` not written, `retire_valid` not raised (it is defaulted low every clock, so no spurious retire). The request is held stable for the whole wait by the combinational driver (`rtl/core/core_io_driver.svh:23-31`): `io_req=1, io_we=q_io_write, io_addr=q_io_port, io_size=q_io_w, io_wdata=<EAX masked>` whenever `state==S_IO`, all from registers latched once at `S_DECODE` (`core_fetch_decode.svh:61-62`) and frozen across the stall. On the cycle `io_ack` asserts, the width-masked IN writeback runs, `eip<=next_eip` (`= q_pc + q_len`, stable across any stall length), and one record retires. Nothing pulls the FSM out of `S_IO`: interrupts are sampled only in `S_DECODE`, and the only override (`+VEN_L1_AXI` fatal `bus_err`→`S_HALT`, `core.sv:3584-3588`) is inert for IO and absent from the default build. `S_INS` is identical per-element; the degenerate `REP INS, ECX==0` case suppresses `io_req` and retires a no-op.

**Verdict (VERIFIED):** the PL→PS IO bridge holds `io_ack=0` until the PS responds, then pulses `io_ack=1` with `io_rdata` valid for one cycle. **Zero RTL change to the IO FSM.**

### 1.2 int-0x80 proxy: ASSUMES SAME-CYCLE — REQUIRES a gated stall (adversarially verified)

The entire proxy commit is the `S_DECODE` arm `else if (proxy_en && d_int80)` (`core_fetch_decode.svh:269-282`). On the **single** `syscall_active` edge (`syscall_active_c = (state==S_DECODE)&&d_int80&&proxy_en`, `core.sv:2153`) it unconditionally latches `gpr[0]<=syscall_eax`, optionally `gs_base_r<=syscall_gs_base`, steps `eip<=eip+d_len`, arms the fold, and leaves to `S_FETCH`. There is **no** ready/valid gate — a repo-wide grep for `syscall_ready|syscall_resp_valid|S_SYSCALL|proxy_ready|VEN_PS_PROXY` returns nothing. With microsecond PS latency the core would latch garbage into EAX/gs and run the folded instruction on a wrong `eax` — silent corruption of the Quake user-mode path.

**Verdict (VERIFIED):** a gated stall state is **required**. The fix mirrors the proven `S_IO` discipline.

### 1.3 Memory (AXI4 → PS-DDR): already real-latency-correct

The `+VEN_L1_AXI` path arms MISS-STALL via `real_bus` and parks `S_HALT` on a fatal `bus_err`. The proven HPC0 datapath needs **no change**; carveout `0x4000_0000/0x1000_0000`, `REMAP_BASE=0x4000_0000`.

---

## 2. Naming conventions (conflict resolution across sections)

The sections proposed divergent names. **Authoritative choices** (matching the adversarial verdict's `required_rtl_changes`):

| Concept | Authoritative name |
|---|---|
| Core-build define for the whole KV260 system top + soc_en=1 + control-plane wiring | `VEN_KV260_SOC` |
| Proxy stall define (new state + new port + split arm) | `VEN_PS_PROXY` |
| New proxy wait FSM state | `S_SYSCALL_WAIT` |
| New core input strobe (PS has filled response + DDR writes) | `syscall_resp_valid` |
| New AXI-Lite control/IO/syscall slave module | `ven_soc_axil.sv` |
| New full-system BD script | `bd_kv260_soc.tcl` |

`VEN_L1_AXI` remains the gate for the `m_axi`/`l1axi_en`/`flush_all`/`bus_err` surface (unchanged). The default Verilator/cosim build sets **none** of `VEN_KV260_SOC`/`VEN_PS_PROXY`, so all M0–M14 gates stay byte-identical.

---

## 3. PL→PS Port-I/O bridge (the simple, stall-clean seam)

`soc_en=1` on the KV260 core top routes device IN/OUT to `S_IO` (`core_fetch_decode.svh:229-235`), except `out 0xf4`→`S_HALT` (process-exit). The bridge is a **passive AXI-Lite capture/release register file** inside `ven_soc_axil.sv`. On the same single `pl_clk0` as core/L1/HPC0 → **no CDC on the io_* seam**; only the level-high `pl_ps_irq0` crosses to the GIC.

**Handshake FSM (4 states):** `IDLE` → (on `io_req && soc_en`, edge-latch fields, set `PENDING`, raise IRQ) `WAIT_PS` (hold `io_ack=0`) → (on PS write of `IO_CTRL.ACK`) `RELEASE` (register `io_rdata<=IO_RDATA`, pulse `io_ack=1` one cycle) → `DONE` (clear `PENDING`, drop IRQ) → `IDLE`. The single ack cycle is all `S_IO` needs.

---

## 4. PL→PS int-0x80 proxy bridge + the gated `S_SYSCALL_WAIT` (the one RTL change)

### 4.1 The gated RTL change (all behind `+VEN_PS_PROXY`)

1. **New state** in the `[5:0]` enum (`core.sv:578-604`), following the `S_FP_BUSY`/`S_DIV_BUSY`/`S_FEXEC_EX` precedent: `S_SYSCALL_WAIT`.
2. **New input port** `syscall_resp_valid` on `core.sv` (after `:118`) and `ventium_top.sv` (after `:92`), wired to the slave's `SYS_RESP_CTL.resp_valid`.
3. **New holding reg** `q_proxy_len` (latch `d_len` on proxy entry), reset in the reset block.
4. **Split the proxy arm** (`core_fetch_decode.svh:269`): under `VEN_PS_PROXY`, on the `syscall_active` edge do **only** `q_proxy_len<=d_len; state<=S_SYSCALL_WAIT` — commit nothing (`eip` parked at `cd80`, `fold_pending_r=0`, no `gpr[0]`/`gs_base_r` latch). The current zero-latency body (`:270-277`) stays **verbatim in the `else`** so the default build is byte-identical.
5. **New wait arm** (compiled only under `VEN_PS_PROXY`), mirroring `S_IO`'s `if(io_ack)`:
   ```
   S_SYSCALL_WAIT: begin
     if (syscall_resp_valid) begin
       gpr[0] <= syscall_eax;
       if (syscall_apply_gs) gs_base_r <= syscall_gs_base;
       eip <= eip + {28'd0, q_proxy_len};
       fold_pending_r <= 1'b1; fold_pc_r <= eip;
       fetch_word <= 3'd0; state <= S_FETCH;
     end
     // else: spin, consume nothing
   end
   ```

**Why correct & minimal:** the core parks with `eip` at `cd80`, fold disarmed, and `cn` (which drives `syscall_n`) frozen because the retire counter (`core.sv:3404-3407`) doesn't tick until the folded retire — so the request `n`/args the PS keys on are stable across the whole microsecond wait. `fold_pc_r<=eip` still stamps the int's pc correctly. The slave **edge-latches** `syscall_n`/`syscall_arg_*` on the `syscall_active` pulse (they are combinational `gpr[]` reads, valid only that edge) and also exposes `state==S_SYSCALL_WAIT` as a sticky `req_pending` level so a polled/interrupt PS cannot miss the 1-clock pulse.

System-mode (FreeDOS/real-mode) is untouched: `proxy_en=0` forces `syscall_active_c=0` and routes `int 0x80` to the loud HALT; `S_SYSCALL_WAIT` is reached only on the Quake user-mode path.

---

## 5. PS→PL control plane (reset / run / config / status) in the same `ven_soc_axil.sv`

The slave also carries the boot-config and status seam. **Core reset = `peripheral_aresetn & CTRL.CORE_RUN`** (synchronous, single `pl_clk0` domain) so the PS holds the core in reset (CORE_RUN resets to 0) while it stages DDR and writes config, then releases. Config (`init_eip`/`init_esp`/`boot_mode`) is latched only at the reset edge (`core.sv:3303/3305/3269`); the mode bits (`cosim_en`/`proxy_en`/`cycle_mode`/`errata_en`/`bus_mode`/`l1axi_en`) are read live for the whole run. AXI-Lite single-master write ordering guarantees CORE_RUN lands after the config writes — **no HW interlock needed** (software contract: don't rewrite config while CORE_RUN=1). The retire counter reuses the **existing** `retire_n` (`ventium_top.sv:300/370`, today DPI-only), tapped to two RO registers with HI-snapshot-on-LO-read to avoid torn 64-bit reads.

### 5.1 Unified AXI-Lite register map (HPM0 base `0xA000_0000`, 64 KiB)

| Offset | Name | Acc | Bits / meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `0x00` | `CTRL` | RW | [0] CORE_RUN (gates rst_n) ; [1] FLUSH_ALL_REQ (W1P→`flush_all`) ; [2] CORE_RST_REQ (W1P) ; [8] IRQ_EN |
| `0x04` | `STATUS` | RO | [0] CPU_HUNG (`:77`) ; [1] BUS_ERR (`:170`, 0 w/o L1_AXI) ; [2] IN_RESET ; [8] IO_REQ ; [9] SYSCALL_PENDING (sticky) |
| `0x08` | `INIT_EIP` | RW | →`init_eip` |
| `0x0C` | `INIT_ESP` | RW | →`init_esp` |
| `0x10` | `MODE` | RW | [0] boot_mode ; [1] cycle_mode ; [2] bus_mode ; [3] l1axi_en ; [4] proxy_en ; [5] cosim_en ; [12:8] errata_en[4:0] |
| `0x14` | `RETIRE_LO` | RO | `retire_n[31:0]` (snapshots HI) |
| `0x18` | `RETIRE_HI` | RO | `retire_n[63:32]` |
| `0x20` | `IO_STATUS` | RO | [0] PENDING ; [1] IS_WRITE(`io_we`) ; [2] BUSY ; [3] IS_INS |
| `0x24` | `IO_ADDR` | RO | [15:0] captured `io_addr` |
| `0x28` | `IO_SIZE` | RO | [2:0] captured `io_size` |
| `0x2C` | `IO_WDATA` | RO | [31:0] captured `io_wdata` (OUT datum) |
| `0x30` | `IO_RDATA` | RW | [31:0] PS writes IN return value |
| `0x34` | `IO_CTRL` | W1P | [0] ACK (commit RDATA + pulse io_ack) ; [1] IRQ_CLR |
| `0x40` | `SYS_N_LO/HI` | RO | `syscall_n[63:0]` (edge-latched) |
| `0x44`–`0x5C` | `SYS_ARG_EAX..EBP` | RO | 7× `syscall_arg_*` (edge-latched) |
| `0x60` | `SYS_RET_EAX` | RW | →`syscall_eax` |
| `0x64` | `SYS_GS` | RW | [0] apply_gs ; [31:0]→`syscall_gs_base` (next reg) |
| `0x68` | `SYS_GS_BASE` | RW | →`syscall_gs_base` |
| `0x6C` | `SYS_RESP_CTL` | W1P | [0] resp_valid (→`syscall_resp_valid`, releases S_SYSCALL_WAIT) ; [1] flush_l1 (mode-2) |
| `0x70` | `IRQ_STATUS` | R/W1C | OR of (IO PENDING \| SYSCALL_PENDING \| CPU_HUNG) |
| `0x7C` | `IDENT` | RO | `0x56544D43` ("VTMC") |

---

## 6. Full-system Vivado BD (`bd_kv260_soc.tcl`, extends `bd_l1axi.tcl`)

Keep the HPC0 coherent DDR datapath (PS8 + SmartConnect 32→128 → `S_AXI_HPC0_FPD`, AFI coherent, one `pl_clk0`) **byte-identical** from `bd_l1axi.tcl:56-122`. Changes:

1. Swap the `ventium_l1_axi_top` Module Reference for a `ventium_top` Module Reference (thin `X_INTERFACE` wrapper to infer `m_axi`); set `REMAP_BASE=0x4000_0000` (cosim hardcodes 0, wrong on KV260 where 0x0 is the PS kernel).
2. Re-enable HPM0: `CONFIG.PSU__USE__M_AXI_GP0 {1}` (was 0 at `:65`), `MAXIGP0__DATA_WIDTH {32}`, clock `maxihpm0_fpd_aclk = pl_clk0`; AXI-Lite SmartConnect `ps8/M_AXI_HPM0_FPD` → `ven_soc_axil/s_axil`, address `0xA000_0000`.
3. Enable `PSU__USE__IRQ0 {1}`; wire `ven_soc_axil/irq_out` → `ps8/pl_ps_irq0[0]`.
4. Replace the `flush_tie` (`:88`) with a PS-driven W1P `flush_all` from `CTRL.FLUSH_ALL_REQ`; wire `bus_err`/`cpu_hung` to slave status.
5. Single `pl_clk0`/`peripheral_aresetn` to core, both SmartConnects, and the slave (CDC_BYPASS carries over).

Keep `validate_bd_design` (0 errors/criticals) and clean `synth_design` DRC bars verbatim; every `ventium_top` input is now driven by the slave (no dangling-input criticals).

### Memory map
| Region | PS view | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| PS kernel / ATF / Linux | `0x0000_0000`–`0x3FFF_FFFF` | A53 |
| Core DDR carveout (program/BIOS + Quake framebuffer) | `0x4000_0000`–`0x4FFF_FFFF` | shared via HPC0 (coherent) |
| Control/IO/syscall slave | `0xA000_0000`–`0xA000_FFFF` | A53 (HPM0) |

No VGA in PL: the framebuffer is a sub-window of the carveout the core writes via HPC0; the A53 reads it coherently (CCI/AFI snoop) and blits.

---

## 7. PS-side software (A53, PetaLinux userspace)

**PetaLinux userspace process, not bare-metal** — `SyscallEmulator` does real host file I/O (`fopen`/`fread` against rootfs, e.g. Quake's `pak0.pak`) which needs a filesystem; the carveout is `mmap`'d via `/dev/mem`/UIO into the same physical DRAM the PL master uses. The app ports three cosim services:

- **`service_io()`** (`tb_main.cpp:482-521`) → ISR/poll on `IO_STATUS.PENDING`: emulate the device (UART/PIT/PIC/IDE/VGA/RTC/kbd — **live emulation**, not the cosim's `dev_in[]` replay), write `IO_RDATA` for an IN, write `IO_CTRL.ACK`.
- **`service_proxy()` emulator path** (`SyscallEmulator::service`, `tb_main.cpp:557-576`) → on `SYSCALL_PENDING`: read `SYS_N_*`/`SYS_ARG_*`, run the emulator, write kernel-effect memory into the carveout, **memory barrier (DSB)**, fill `SYS_RET_EAX`/`SYS_GS*`, write `SYS_RESP_CTL.resp_valid`. (`MemModel` swapped for the mmap'd pointer; synthetic clock → real `CNTVCT`.)
- **Loader** (`load_quake_image` / system-image loader): stage image into the carveout, flush, then release reset.

`service_bus()`/`axi_drive()` is **deleted** — the carveout IS the memory via HPC0. The DPI retire trace is **not consumed** on HW; the oracle is the program's own output (stdout/framebuffer). Termination: `out 0xf4` / `exit_group` / `cpu_hung`-observe-and-reset. **Interrupt-driven with an adaptive poll window** (default sleep on GIC; spin briefly under dense F4 syscall traffic) — correctness is poll/IRQ-agnostic because the core waits indefinitely.

---

## 8. Default-build byte-identity guarantee

`ven_soc_axil.sv`, the `soc_en=1` top instantiation, the HPM0 wiring, the `core_rst_n` derivation, the retire taps, and all `S_SYSCALL_WAIT`/`syscall_resp_valid`/`q_proxy_len` additions are behind `VEN_KV260_SOC`/`VEN_PS_PROXY` and live in **new files** or `ifdef` blocks. `ventium_top.sv` keeps `soc_en=1'b0`. The default Verilator cosim build sets neither define, so the enum, ports, proxy arm, IO seam, and `retire_n`→DPI are exactly today's RTL → all M0–M14 gates stay bit-for-bit.

---

## 9. IMPLEMENTATION PLAN — ordered deliverables to F2→F3→F4

The IO seam (F2/F3) is **independent** of the proxy seam (F4) and is already stall-clean, so it ships first and de-risks the single RTL dependency. Order:

### Phase A — bring-up & F2 (boot)

**A1 (THE SINGLE BEST FIRST RTL DELIVERABLE): `rtl/soc/ven_soc_axil.sv`** — the AXI4-Lite control/IO slave (control + status + retire + IO capture/release register file of §5.1, IO offsets `0x00`–`0x34` + `0x70`–`0x7C`). Pure new file, no existing source touched.
   - *+define gate:* none on the file (compiled only into the KV260 filelist); referenced under `VEN_KV260_SOC` in the top.
   - *Unit gate:* standalone testbench (`verif/unit/tb_ven_soc_axil.sv`) — AXI-Lite read/write of every register; the IO capture→hold-`io_ack=0`→ACK→one-cycle-`io_ack` pulse handshake (SVA: `io_ack` is exactly one `pl_clk0` wide per ACK); `core_rst_n = aresetn & CORE_RUN`; IDENT reads `0x56544D43`.
   - *Why first:* it is the entire PS↔PL bridge for IO + reset + config + status, needs **zero** core changes (the IO stall is already correct), and unblocks both the BD and the F2 boot. Everything else depends on this seam existing.

**A2: KV260 core top + `soc_en=1` wiring** — new `rtl/ventium_kv260_top.sv` (or `VEN_KV260_SOC` block in a wrapper) instantiating `ventium_top` with `soc_en=1` and `ven_soc_axil`; `ventium_top.sv` keeps `soc_en=1'b0`.
   - *Gate:* `+VEN_KV260_SOC` (with `+VEN_L1_AXI`).
   - *Unit gate:* elaboration + a directed IN/OUT-stall sim (drive `io_req`, hold `io_ack` N cycles via the slave, confirm EAX writeback and single retire after late ACK).

**A3: `fpga/scripts/bd_kv260_soc.tcl`** — §6 BD (HPM0 re-enable + slave + IRQ + `ventium_top` MR + `REMAP_BASE`).
   - *Gate:* full-system BD (default cosim never runs Vivado).
   - *Unit gate:* `validate_bd_design` 0 errors/criticals + clean `synth_design` DRC.

**A4: PS app v0 (`sw/ps/ven_soc_app`)** — loader + reset/boot sequencing + `service_io()` (UART out, minimal PIT/PIC, `out 0xf4` exit) + `cpu_hung`-observe-and-reset; interrupt-driven on `pl_ps_irq0`.
   - *F2 gate:* core boots from carveout, prints to console over the emulated UART, exits via `out 0xf4`.

### Phase B — F3 (FreeDOS)

**B1: PS device models** — port IDE/disk + RTC + keyboard register semantics into `service_io()` (reference: M8 `ventium_soc` device behaviors, run as A53 software).
   - *F3 gate:* FreeDOS boots to a prompt over the emulated console; disk reads serviced through the IO stall.

### Phase C — F4 (first Quake frame)

**C1: RTL — `S_SYSCALL_WAIT` + `syscall_resp_valid` + `q_proxy_len`** (§4.1), all behind `+VEN_PS_PROXY`; split proxy arm with the current body verbatim in the `else`.
   - *Gate:* `+VEN_PS_PROXY`.
   - *Unit gate:* (a) `make verify` byte-identical with the define **off** (default build unchanged); (b) with the define **on**, a directed sim that pulses `syscall_active`, holds N cycles, then asserts `syscall_resp_valid` — confirm `gpr[0]<=syscall_eax`, `gs_base_r` latch, `eip` advances by `q_proxy_len`, fold fires, single retire with `fold_pc_r==cd80 pc`; and that `cn`/`syscall_n`/args are frozen across the stall.

**C2: Extend `ven_soc_axil.sv`** — add the syscall window (`SYS_N_*`/`SYS_ARG_*` edge-latched on `syscall_active`; `SYS_RET_EAX`/`SYS_GS*`/`SYS_RESP_CTL`; sticky `SYSCALL_PENDING`).
   - *Unit gate:* SVA that `syscall_resp_valid` is one cycle per `SYS_RESP_CTL.resp_valid` write; args latched on the pulse stay stable until ACK.

**C3: BD update** — wire the new `syscall_resp_valid`/syscall seam ports through `bd_kv260_soc.tcl`. *Gate:* re-validate.

**C4: PS app — proxy path** — switch to user-mode (`proxy_en=1`, image loader), `SyscallEmulator` over the carveout pointer with a DSB before `resp_valid`, and the P5Q1 framebuffer readout (palette+indexed → RGB blit to DRM/framebuffer, or capture to file).
   - *F4 gate:* Quake runs in user-mode; the first frame appears (live blit or captured P5Q1 stream).

### Carried open dependencies (track, not blocking A1)
- Full-system `ventium_top` PL-clock fmax (FP-pipeline note cites ~35 MHz routing-bound post-place) — may force a slower `pl_clk0` or a clock split that would break single-clock CDC_BYPASS and require real CDC on `m_axi`.
- Whether the initial bulk carveout load needs an explicit flush (vs. AFI coherency) before reset release.
- IRQ-vs-poll tuning for F4 Quake syscall density.

---

New files to create: `/home/yukidama/github/ventium/rtl/soc/ven_soc_axil.sv`, `/home/yukidama/github/ventium/rtl/ventium_kv260_top.sv`, `/home/yukidama/github/ventium/fpga/scripts/bd_kv260_soc.tcl`, `/home/yukidama/github/ventium/sw/ps/ven_soc_app/`.
